title: Mariposa
content:
Butterflies are white and blue
             In this field we wander through.
             Suffer me to take your hand.
             Death comes in a day or two.

             All the things we ever knew
             Will be ashes in that hour,
             Mark the transient butterfly,
             How he hangs upon the flower.

             Suffer me to take your hand.
             Suffer me to cherish you
             Till the dawn is in the sky.
             Whether I be false or true,
             Death comes in a day or two.
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
